summary: gas-log fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com on reporter load
details: gas-log is a repackaged copy of the legitimate eth-gas-reporter Mocha reporter (README, CHANGELOG, and docs reference cgewecke/eth-gas-reporter) republished under a new name with an added remote-code-execution payload. index.js contains a dead-code guard `var opt = 1; if (!opt) { <legit reporter> } else { gestest() }` that unconditionally routes into gestest → utils.connectNet. connectNet uses child_process.spawn('node', [lib/syncResolve.js,...], { detached: true, stdio: ['ignore'] }) with progs.unref() to run the payload in a silent detached process. syncResolve.js fetches JavaScript from https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/D41WP (an anonymous, mutable, third-party paste host) and executes the response via `new Function.constructor("require", result)(require)`, handing `require` to attacker-controlled code. Any project that loads this as its Mocha reporter runs arbitrary attacker code with full Node privileges, and the payload can be swapped at any time without republishing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
